case_id: Ridgeline Wind Energy Project, Garrett County, Maryland

pi_items.source: Task requires 8 sections (Project Description, Regulatory Framework, Ecological Resources, Noise Impact, Visual Impact, Water Resources, Mitigation Plan, Conclusions); 7 is reasonable minimum
Multiple subsections expected within each main section for comprehensive EIA; 8 minimum supports structured document
Task description explicitly requires: (a) Wildlife Species Inventory, (b) Noise Receptor Data, (c) Mitigation Measures
Task description explicitly states: 'Synthesize the JSON data into professional narrative text (at least 25 paragraphs)'
Reasonable minimum for ODT file containing 7+ headings, 8+ subheadings, 3 tables, 25+ paragraphs; matches verifier gate condition

Confirmed present in input data (wildlife_species array). Indiana Bat (Myotis sodalis) is real federally endangered species per U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service (https://www.fws.gov/species/indiana-bat-myotis-sodalis)
Confirmed present in input data as scientific name for Indiana Bat. Myotis sodalis is the correct scientific nomenclature per USFWS
Confirmed present in input data (noise_impact_assessment section with dba field values 42.1, 46.8, 37.4, 34.2). dBA (decibels A-weighted) is standard unit for environmental noise assessment
Confirmed present in input data: project_name 'Ridgeline Wind Energy Project' and location 'Backbone Mountain ridgeline, Garrett County, Maryland'

Confirmed present in input data (location field). Garrett County is real county in Maryland, located in Appalachian Mountains, western Maryland (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garrett_County,_Maryland)

pi_items.metadata_value: Vestas V136-4.0 MW (claimed in input data)
pi_items.source: Real turbine model. Vestas V136 is 4 MW platform deployed globally since 2010 with 81+ GW installed (https://www.vestas.com/en/energy-solutions/onshore-wind-turbines/4-mw-platform/V136-4-2-MW)
Real international standard. ISO 9613-2 is International Standard for Acoustics - Attenuation of Sound during Propagation Outdoors, specifically used for wind turbine noise assessment

Standard residential noise limit. EPA and WHO guidance recommend 55 dBA from 7am-10pm for residential areas; 45 dBA from 10pm-7am (https://ehs.yale.edu/sites/default/files/files/decibel-level-chart.pdf)

pi_items.metadata_value: Allegheny Woodrat (Neotoma magister), State Endangered (in input data)
pi_items.source: Real species. State-level endangered status confirmed in multiple states (Pennsylvania, New Jersey). Scientific name correct (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Allegheny_woodrat)

pi_items.metadata_value: Cerulean Warbler (Setophaga cerulea), Species of Concern (in input data)
pi_items.source: Real species. Listed as Endangered in Canada; Species of Concern/Vulnerable/Near Threatened globally. Scientific name correct (https://www.fws.gov/species/cerulean-warbler-dendroica-cerulea)

pi_items.metadata_value: Timber Rattlesnake (Crotalus horridus), State Endangered (in input data)
pi_items.source: Real species. Endangered status in 6 states, Threatened in 5 states. Scientific name correct (https://www.fws.gov/species/timber-rattlesnake-crotalus-horridus)

privileged_info_summary: The task involves creating an Environmental Impact Assessment executive summary for the Ridgeline Wind Energy Project in Garrett County, Maryland using synthetic but realistic data. All metadata requirements are verified: output file path (/home/ga/Documents/Ridgeline_Wind_EIA_Summary.odt), input JSON data containing 8 required sections with 4 wildlife species, noise assessment using ISO 9613-2 standard with 55 dBA limit, and identified keywords (Indiana Bat/Myotis sodalis, dBA, Ridgeline, Garrett County). The document requirements (7 H1 headings, 8 H2 headings, 3 tables for Wildlife/Noise/Mitigation, 25 paragraphs, 5000+ bytes) are reasonable and explicitly stated in task description. All referenced species (Indiana Bat, Allegheny Woodrat, Cerulean Warbler, Timber Rattlesnake) are real endangered or species-of-concern species; Vestas V136-4.0 MW turbine and ISO 9613-2 standard are real; Garrett County is real location in western Maryland; 55 dBA is standard residential noise limit.
